questions.question_text: South Dakota requires a separate license for property management. How many education hours are needed?
distractors: 116 hours of general real estate education
No education is required for PM licensure
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires a separate property management license with 40 hours of Fundamentals of Property Management education and a passing exam score of 75%. This is distinct from the broker associate license.

questions.question_text: A person wants to help tenants find apartments and collect a fee, but not manage properties. What SD license do they need?
distractors: No license is required for this activity
questions.explanation: The residential rental agent license covers limited rental activities. It's unique because it requires no education or examination — only an application and approval. A full property management license (40 hours education + exam) is needed for broader PM duties.

questions.question_text: A property manager in South Dakota collects rental deposits. Where must these funds be held?
questions.correct_answer: In a properly designated trust account
distractors: In the property manager's personal account
In the property owner's bank account directly
In a separate escrow at the title company
questions.explanation: Property managers must handle rental deposits and rents through trust funds, following the same trust account rules that apply to other real estate licensees. Security deposits and rents are other people's money and must be properly safeguarded.

questions.question_text: Before a timeshare project can be sold to the public in South Dakota, it must be:
distractors: Approved by the local zoning board
Listed on the state MLS system
questions.explanation: The SDREC registers timeshare and condominium projects as well as subdivisions before they can be marketed and sold. This registration requirement helps protect consumers from fraudulent or poorly disclosed development offerings.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes the residential rental agent license from the property management license in South Dakota?
questions.correct_answer: Rental agents need no education or exam
distractors: Rental agents can manage larger portfolios
Property managers have fewer regulatory duties
Rental agents must have broker supervision
questions.explanation: The key distinction is that residential rental agents need no education or examination — just application approval. Property managers must complete 40 hours of education and pass an exam. Rental agents are limited to basic rental activities.
